response.justification: While Bogdan has 20 years of strong software engineering experience and current work in AI infrastructure, this is a fundamental domain mismatch. The Principal Performance Architect role requires specialized expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, GPU optimization, and deep learning workload analysis—areas completely absent from the resume. His background is application and service-layer development, not hardware-software co-design or accelerator optimization. The current role's AI infrastructure work appears to be tooling/frameworks, not the hardware-level performance optimization this position demands.
strengths: 20 years of professional software development with demonstrable progression to Staff/Principal roles at reputable companies
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, microservices architecture) and ability to optimize software systems for performance at the application layer
Current role at Naver includes AI-agent infrastructure work, showing some exposure to AI technology domain
concerns: Completely lacks required SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture expertise—no background in hardware design or chip-level optimization
No experience with GPU programming, accelerator optimization, or deep learning workload analysis; AI work appears focused on infrastructure tooling rather than ML optimization
Career trajectory is entirely application/service software, not systems architecture—never worked on hardware-software co-design or performance modeling at the hardware level

key_factors.factor: SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture knowledge
General seniority and software engineering track record
